name: Paint
version_note: Windows 11 Paint (Cocreator) — NOT classic Paint. Dark mode enabled. No ribbon, minimal toolbar.

tools.Pencil: UIA name 'Pencil' — freehand drawing tool. In toolbar at ~y=131.
tools.Fill with color: UIA name 'Fill with color' — flood fill tool. Use fill_at(x,y) which clamps to canvas bounds. Good for filling backgrounds and large areas.
tools.Text: UIA name 'Text' — click then click canvas to place text box
tools.Shapes: UIA name 'Shapes' — opens the shapes gallery/dropdown with Ellipse, Rectangle, Triangle, Line, etc.
tools.Ellipse: Inside Shapes gallery — click 'Shapes' first to expand, then click 'Ellipse'. Use Shift+drag for perfect circle.
tools.Rectangle: Inside Shapes gallery — click 'Shapes' first to expand, then click 'Rectangle'.
tools.Line: Inside Shapes gallery — click 'Shapes' first, then 'Line'.
tools.Triangle: Inside Shapes gallery — click 'Shapes' first, then 'Triangle'.
tools.Brushes: UIA name 'Brushes' — dropdown with brush variants

colors.note: New Paint has small color circles in the toolbar area (~y=125-140). ONLY use these 11 fast toolbar colors: black, white, red, green, blue, yellow, orange, purple, pink, brown, gray. Non-standard colors (lightblue, darkblue, peach, etc.) are auto-remapped to nearest fast color. The Edit Colors dialog DOES NOT WORK reliably in Win11 Paint — it freezes the app.
colors.method: paint_select_color() uses UIA element lookup to click toolbar swatches. Non-standard colors are remapped to nearest fast color. Edit Colors dialog is NEVER used.

canvas.background_color: RGB(254,184,112) in dark mode — NOT white
canvas.detection: get_canvas_bounds() uses pixel scanning. Detects both white (light mode) and warm-toned (dark mode) canvas.
canvas.note: Canvas is NOT the full window. Always use get_canvas_bounds() to get exact coordinates.

drawing.method: smoothDrag via clawmetheus HTTP API using Win32 mouse_event for reliable input.
drawing.canvas_activation: All draw_* functions activate the canvas automatically by clicking it before drawing. No manual activation needed.
drawing.speed: Default speed is 200. Keep <= 300. Filled shapes use speed 400 internally.

primitives.draw_rect: Rectangle outline as single continuous path
primitives.draw_ellipse: Ellipse outline with separate rx/ry radii
primitives.draw_polygon: Closed polygon through list of points
primitives.draw_curve: Smooth Catmull-Rom curve through control points
primitives.draw_rays: Radial lines from inner to outer radius
primitives.draw_filled_rect: Filled rectangle via pencil hatching — SLOW (~45s for 300px). Use Paint shape tools instead.
primitives.draw_filled_circle: Filled circle via pencil hatching — SLOW (~60s for 200px radius). Use Paint shape tools instead.

drawing.pattern: 1) select_color(), 2) ensure_foreground(), 3) use_pencil(), 4) draw_*(). Group by color to minimize switches.

known_issues: Canvas background is RGB(254,184,112) in dark mode — NOT white. _find_canvas_by_pixels handles both.
Edit Colors dialog CRASHES Win11 Paint — NEVER use set_color_rgb(). Only use fast toolbar colors via paint_select_color().
Canvas resize handles at edges — stay 15px away from all canvas borders.
Ctrl+W opens Resize and Skew dialog, NOT close. Use kill_app() or Alt+F4 to close Paint.
If Paint is NOT maximized, toolbar coordinates differ. Always maximize before drawing.
Ctrl+S saves in-place if file was previously saved. Use F12 for Save As.
Save As dialog: filename field is pre-focused. Do NOT use Ctrl+A in Save As.
After Ctrl+N (new canvas), a 'Save changes?' dialog may appear. Press 'N' for Don't Save.
Shape tools are inside 'Shapes' gallery dropdown. Click 'Shapes' first to expand, THEN click the shape.
find_tool('Ellipse') may return off-screen coordinates (Y=-20011). Always validate coords are on-screen.
pyautogui.mouseDown/moveTo does NOT draw in Paint. Only clawmetheus smoothDrag works.
After clicking a toolbar button, you MUST click the canvas before smoothDrag will draw.
Screen resolution may not be 1920x1080. Use get_screen_size() or pyautogui.size().
key() only accepts positional args: key('enter'), key('ctrl','s'). NO keyword args.
draw_filled_rect/draw_filled_circle are EXTREMELY SLOW (30-60s each). Use Paint shape tools for filled shapes instead.
Scripts timeout at 300s. Max ~30 draw_* calls per script. Keep it simple.
tips: ALWAYS use get_canvas_bounds() to detect canvas area.
ALWAYS maximize Paint before drawing: ensure_maximized('Paint')
Use select_color('red') for colors — tries toolbar swatches first, falls back to Edit Colors dialog.
Use app_save(filepath, 'Paint') to save — handles Save As dialog automatically.
Use new_canvas() to reset to blank instead of kill+reopen.
For drawing: pick color FIRST with select_color(), THEN use_pencil(), THEN draw_circle/draw_line.

For FILLED shapes (face, body, eyes): use Paint shape tools (paint_shape_tool + paint_fill_style + paint_draw_shape). NEVER use pencil outline + fill_at — pencil lines have gaps that cause fill to leak everywhere.
If fill leaks and floods the canvas, DO NOT undo. Just continue drawing over it or use Ctrl+Z ONCE at most.
NEVER use flood fill (use_fill/fill_at) on pencil-drawn shapes — pixel gaps cause fill to leak.
ALL paint actions self-validate: they check foreground, tool state, and canvas bounds automatically. No need for separate verification steps.
paint_draw auto-selects pencil if not active. paint_fill_at auto-selects fill tool if not active.
paint_draw_shape is for shape tools — validates that a shape tool is selected before dragging.
draw_circle() handles canvas activation internally — no need to click canvas before calling it.
Default smoothDrag speed is 200. Keep it <= 300.
Plan the composition before drawing. Draw each element EXACTLY ONCE.

For FILLED shapes: use Paint's shape tools (Shapes → Ellipse/Rectangle + set_fill('Solid color') + paint_draw_shape). NEVER use draw_filled_rect/draw_filled_circle for large shapes — they take 30-60s each.
HARD TIME LIMIT: Scripts timeout at 300s. Keep drawings to 10-20 shapes max. Each draw_* call takes 2-5s.
Successful sequence: paint_color → paint_fill_tool → paint_fill_at → paint_color → paint_shape_tool
Successful sequence: paint_color → paint_shape_tool → paint_fill_style → paint_outline_style → paint_draw_shape
